alternatives_considered: Keep original v0.9 scope (dual-output capture + Cursor) — rejected: Tier 3 work for one runtime vs Tier 1 for all runtimes
Ship MCP + dual-output together — rejected: scope creep, delays ship date
content.context: After MCP architecture pivot, realized Tier 1 (MCP recall) delivers 80% of first-contact user value with fraction of the effort of Tier 3 (adapter+MRP per runtime). Bootstrap already shipped in v0.8 — only missing piece for the 5-min-to-value UX is the MCP server.
content.decision: v0.9.0 theme reshuffled to 'MCP server + Tier 1 integration + bootstrap polish'. Dual-output capture, Cursor adapter, and auto-capture hooks moved to v0.10.
impact: v0.9 issues: #81 (MCP server), #80 (bootstrap UX polish), #77 (multi-repo walk), #79 (AST improvements)
v0.10 absorbs: dual-output capture, Cursor adapter, hook-based auto-capture, TUI monitor
Outreach wave paired with v0.9 ship: PRs to openclaude + cline, blog post, HN push
content.why: Ship breadth before depth. MCP server + bootstrap polish unlocks every MCP-aware runtime simultaneously. Tier 3 depth (auto-capture, adapter-specific hooks) adds value for power users after they've already adopted via Tier 1. Killer UX: mom bootstrap . && mom serve --mcp → any runtime queries codebase memories in 5 minutes.
